## Identity

Harwin and Jonna's son, gen-4, called "the Eel King" — a title he adopted
in earnest, unaware (or unbothered) that his great-aunt Myrcella had already
worn it as a joke. He borrowed against the salt tithe to hire the Brined
Company for a war he could not afford, and the war killed him for it.

## Quotes

> "They can call me the Eel King. Kings borrow. That's what makes them
> kings."

— Ormund Quorwyn, `sources/chapters/asalt-ormund-01.md:44`

> "The tithe will cover it. The tithe always covers it, until the year it
> doesn't, and I intend to be dead before that year."

— Ormund Quorwyn, `sources/chapters/asalt-ormund-02.md:9`
